rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical tandem radical addition/cyclization of para-quinone methides with haloacetonitriles to construct spiro[cyclohexane-1,2'-indene]-2,5-dien-4-one derivatives
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of chloroacetonitrile generates a cyanomethyl radical and chloride anion; the chloride anion is then oxidized at the anode to a chlorine radical that couples with the carbon-centered intermediate

key_intermediates.name: cyanomethyl radical (·CH2CN) from cathodic reduction of ClCH2CN
Radical intermediate A from addition of ·CH2CN to alkyne of p-QM 1a
Indene-type C-centered radical B after 5-endo-dig cyclization (favored)
Alternative radical B' (disfavored due to steric hindrance)
Chlorine radical from anodic oxidation of Cl-

electron_flow: Chloroacetonitrile is reduced at the cathode to give a cyanomethyl radical plus chloride; the cyanomethyl radical adds to the alkyne of the p-quinone methide and undergoes 5-endo-dig cyclization to vinyl radical B; meanwhile chloride is oxidized at the anode to a chlorine radical, which couples with B to deliver the (Z)-spiro product 3aa.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO (2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-1-piperidinyloxy)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited with 2.0 equiv TEMPO, indicating radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited with 2.0 equiv BHT, supporting radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of chloroacetonitrile 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Cathodic reduction peak at -0.992 V shows 2a can accept electrons at the cathode

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of NaCl
probe_reagents.observation: Anodic oxidation peak at +1.23 V shows chloride can lose electrons at the anode

notes: Z-selectivity rationalized by preferred formation of intermediate B over sterically disfavored B'; the (E)-isomer pathway via B' is suppressed by steric hindrance. X-ray of product 3af confirms structure. Scaled-up reaction (8 mmol) gave 3ca in 62% yield; product can be elaborated by Sonogashira coupling.
